evals.name: text-fresh-first-fixture
evals.prompt: I just got a real-world KVR file from a customer and want to harden the parser against it. Add `./text-fresh/sample.kvr` as a golden test fixture for the Go file library package at `./text-fresh/kvr`. The package is freshly scaffolded — there's no `testdata/` directory yet and no fixture-driven test wired up. Wire up a round-trip test that reads from `testdata/`, parses the file, prints, parses the output, and asserts the two ASTs are equal.
evals.expected_output: ./text-fresh/kvr/testdata/sample.kvr exists with bytes identical to ./text-fresh/sample.kvr. ./text-fresh/kvr/printer_test.go contains a new TestRoundTripFromTestdata function with one entry referencing 'sample.kvr', using the Parse → Print → Parse → require.Equal pattern (AST equality, not byte equality). The package builds and tests pass (the kvr stub returns &File{} for any input, so the round-trip is vacuously correct).

assertions.text: text-fresh/kvr/testdata/sample.kvr exists
text-fresh/kvr/testdata/sample.kvr is byte-identical to the original text-fresh/sample.kvr
text-fresh/kvr/testdata/ directory exists (was not present in the fixture)
text-fresh/kvr/printer_test.go contains a new function named TestRoundTripFromTestdata
TestRoundTripFromTestdata reads the fixture via os.ReadFile and a path under testdata/
TestRoundTripFromTestdata asserts AST equality (require.Equal on two *File values), not byte equality on the printed output
TestRoundTripFromTestdata follows the Parse(file) → Print → Parse(buf) → require.Equal(first, second) shape
TestRoundTripFromTestdata is table-driven with a testCases slice and t.Run subtests
TestRoundTripFromTestdata calls t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
TestRoundTripFromTestdata uses github.com/stretchr/testify/require for assertions
`go build ./...` succeeds in text-fresh/kvr/
`go test -race ./...` passes in text-fresh/kvr/ (the new round-trip case passes vacuously against the stub)
The pre-existing TestPrinter and TestPrinterRoundTrip tests in printer_test.go are unchanged from the fixture
tokenizer.go, parser.go, printer.go in text-fresh/kvr/ are unchanged from the fixture (skill is additive only)
SPEC.md in text-fresh/kvr/ is unchanged from the fixture

evals.name: binary-fresh-first-fixture
evals.prompt: I have a real-world TLV-encoded blob at `./binary-fresh/sample.tlv` and want it pinned as a regression-guard fixture for my Go binary file library at `./binary-fresh/tlv`. The package is freshly scaffolded (types.go / decoder.go / encoder.go stubs); there's no `testdata/` yet. Drop the file in as a golden fixture and wire a round-trip test against it. For binary I want byte-equality on the round-trip, not AST equality.
evals.expected_output: ./binary-fresh/tlv/testdata/sample.tlv exists with bytes identical to ./binary-fresh/sample.tlv. ./binary-fresh/tlv/encoder_test.go contains a new TestRoundTripFromTestdata function using the Decode(file) → Encode → require.Equal(originalBytes, buf.Bytes()) pattern (byte equality). The package builds. The new round-trip test will fail with errUnimplemented because the decoder is still stubbed — that's expected and the skill should report it as an implementation gap, not as a skill bug.

assertions.text: binary-fresh/tlv/testdata/sample.tlv exists
binary-fresh/tlv/testdata/sample.tlv is byte-identical to the original binary-fresh/sample.tlv
binary-fresh/tlv/testdata/ directory exists (was not present in the fixture)
binary-fresh/tlv/encoder_test.go contains a new function named TestRoundTripFromTestdata
TestRoundTripFromTestdata reads the fixture via os.ReadFile and a path under testdata/
TestRoundTripFromTestdata asserts byte equality (require.Equal on the original bytes vs encoded bytes), NOT AST equality on *File values
TestRoundTripFromTestdata follows the Decode(file) → Encode(&buf, f) → require.Equal(originalBytes, buf.Bytes()) shape
TestRoundTripFromTestdata is table-driven with a testCases slice and t.Run subtests
TestRoundTripFromTestdata calls t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
TestRoundTripFromTestdata uses github.com/stretchr/testify/require for assertions
`go build ./...` succeeds in binary-fresh/tlv/ (test wiring compiles cleanly)

The skill's final user-facing report mentions the new round-trip test failing (or errUnimplemented), and frames the failure as an implementation gap to fix next — not as a skill bug. The skill does NOT claim 'tests pass' if they don't.
The pre-existing TestDecodeStubReturnsErrUnimplemented and TestEncodeStubReturnsErrUnimplemented tests are unchanged from the fixture
types.go, decoder.go, encoder.go in binary-fresh/tlv/ are unchanged from the fixture (skill is additive only)

evals.name: text-second-append-fixture
evals.prompt: Add `./text-second/sample2.kvr` as another golden fixture for the kvr package at `./text-second/kvr`. The package already has one fixture wired up (testdata/example.kvr referenced from TestRoundTripFromTestdata in printer_test.go) — append the new one to the existing test table rather than creating a duplicate test function.
evals.expected_output: ./text-second/kvr/testdata/sample2.kvr exists with bytes identical to ./text-second/sample2.kvr. ./text-second/kvr/printer_test.go's existing TestRoundTripFromTestdata function gains a second testCases entry referencing 'sample2.kvr'; the existing 'example_kvr' / 'example.kvr' entry is preserved unchanged. No new TestRoundTripFromTestdata function is created. The package builds and tests pass.

assertions.text: text-second/kvr/testdata/sample2.kvr exists
text-second/kvr/testdata/sample2.kvr is byte-identical to the original text-second/sample2.kvr
text-second/kvr/testdata/example.kvr is unchanged from the fixture
text-second/kvr/printer_test.go contains exactly one TestRoundTripFromTestdata function (no duplicates, no _2 suffix)
The pre-existing testCases entry for example.kvr (slug 'example_kvr') is still present in TestRoundTripFromTestdata
TestRoundTripFromTestdata's testCases slice has a new entry referencing 'sample2.kvr'
TestRoundTripFromTestdata's testCases slice now contains exactly two entries
The body of TestRoundTripFromTestdata's t.Run loop (Parse → Print → Parse → require.Equal) is unchanged from the fixture
`go build ./...` succeeds in text-second/kvr/
`go test -race ./...` passes in text-second/kvr/ (both the original and new round-trip cases pass vacuously against the stub)
tokenizer.go, parser.go, printer.go in text-second/kvr/ are unchanged from the fixture (skill is additive only)
TestPrinter and TestPrinterRoundTrip in printer_test.go are unchanged from the fixture
